steps.title: Find the contrast signal
steps.content: The word sharpened is the decisive clue. Recent analyses have sharpened (clarified) our knowledge of hominids, so the knowledge was once in the opposite condition: unclear, hazy, or vague.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean hazy or unclear — the opposite of "sharpened," which is what recent analyses did to our knowledge. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — unprecedented: "our knowledge was once unprecedented" — unprecedented means never seen before; this doesn't contrast with being sharpened or clarified. ✗
  • Choice B — nebulous: "our knowledge was once nebulous" — nebulous means vague and hazy, the direct opposite of sharp and clear. ✓
  • Choice C — intricate: "our knowledge was once intricate" — intricate means highly complex or detailed; detailed knowledge wouldn't need to be sharpened into clarity. ✗
  • Choice D — concrete: "our knowledge was once concrete" — concrete means definite and tangible; if it were already concrete, recent analyses wouldn't need to sharpen it. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
